title: `podman machine` time drift
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Leaving the podman VM running will cause a time drift pretty quickly, ~5 min after two days.

```sh
$ podman machine ssh 'uname -a ; date -u && uptime --pretty && exit' 2>/dev/null; echo ; date -u && podman --version && uname -sri
Linux localhost.localdomain 5.15.6-200.fc35.x86_64 #1 SMP Wed Dec 1 13:41:10 UTC 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
Fri Dec 10 13:04:43 UTC 2021
up 2 days, 7 hours, 29 minutes

Fri Dec 10 13:09:53 UTC 2021
podman version 3.4.2
Darwin 20.6.0 MacBookPro14,1
```

One possible issue is to add NTP servers to the `systemd-timesyncd` service.
Will report back after the weekend if this hasn't worked.

```sh
[core@localhost ~]$ sudo sed -i 's/#NTP=.*/NTP=0.fedora.pool.ntp.org 1.fedora.pool.ntp.org/g' /etc/systemd/timesyncd.conf 
[core@localhost ~]$ grep -v '^#' /etc/systemd/timesyncd.conf 

[Time]
NTP=0.fedora.pool.ntp.org 1.fedora.pool.ntp.org
[core@localhost ~]$ sudo systemctl restart systemd-timesyncd.service 
```

```sh
$ podman machine ssh 'uname -a ; date -u && uptime --pretty && exit' 2>/dev/null; echo ; date -u && podman --version && uname -sri
Linux localhost.localdomain 5.15.6-200.fc35.x86_64 #1 SMP Wed Dec 1 13:41:10 UTC 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
Fri Dec 10 13:12:33 UTC 2021
up 2 days, 7 hours, 32 minutes

Fri Dec 10 13:12:33 UTC 2021
podman version 3.4.2
Darwin 20.6.0 MacBookPro14,1
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman machine init`

2. `podman machine start`

3. _wait_

**Describe the results you received:**

The podman VM time is drifting.

**Describe the results you expected:**

The podman VM time is not drifting.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Client:
Version:      3.4.2
API Version:  3.4.2
Go Version:   go1.17.2
Built:        Fri Nov 12 17:08:25 2021
OS/Arch:      darwin/amd64

Server:
Version:      3.4.2
API Version:  3.4.2
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Fri Nov 12 21:25:37 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes
